headline: US wireless carriers to launch joint venture to address rural 'dead zones'
summary: Verizon, AT&T ‌and T-Mobile said ‌on Thursday that they ​agreed in principle to form a new joint venture ‌that ⁠seeks to address long-time coverage gaps ⁠especially in rural areas by ​using ​satellite-based ​technologies. The largest ‌U.S. wireless companies said the plan seeks to end nearly all dead zones ‌without mobile ​service. The ​plan also aims ‌to ensure redundant ​connectivity ​during natural disasters and improved ​network ‌performance.

content.title: US wireless carriers to launch joint venture to address rural 'dead zones'
content.summary: Verizon, AT&T ‌and T-Mobile said ‌on Thursday that they ​agreed in principle to form a new joint venture ‌that ⁠seeks to address long-time coverage gaps ⁠especially in rural areas by ​using ​satellite-based ​technologies. The largest ‌U.S. wireless companies said the plan seeks to end nearly all dead zones ‌without mobile ​service. The ​plan also aims ‌to ensure redundant ​connectivity ​during natural disasters and improved ​network ‌performance.

thumbnail.caption: The AT&T logo in Mexico City

headline: AST SpaceMobile Commends Proposed Direct-to-Device Joint Venture by U.S. Mobile Network Operators
summary: MIDLAND, Texas, May 14, 2026--AST SpaceMobile, Inc. ("AST SpaceMobile") (NASDAQ: ASTS), the company building the first and only space-based cellular broadband network accessible directly by everyday smartphones, designed for both commercial and government applications, today commends the announcement by AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon to form a joint venture to extend mobile connectivity for wireless customers by using satellite-based, direct-to-service technologies.

content.title: AST SpaceMobile Commends Proposed Direct-to-Device Joint Venture by U.S. Mobile Network Operators
content.summary: MIDLAND, Texas, May 14, 2026--AST SpaceMobile, Inc. ("AST SpaceMobile") (NASDAQ: ASTS), the company building the first and only space-based cellular broadband network accessible directly by everyday smartphones, designed for both commercial and government applications, today commends the announcement by AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon to form a joint venture to extend mobile connectivity for wireless customers by using satellite-based, direct-to-service technologies.

headline: AST SpaceMobile Stock Pops. Thank AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile.
summary: AST SpaceMobile  stock was rising early Thursday.  A joint venture between major cell carriers to boost coverage in remote areas could be good news for the satellite-broadband stock.  AT&T Verizon Communications  and  T-Mobile US   said Thursday they are forming a joint venture to help end dead zones in the U.S. by the use of satellite-based wireless networks.

content.title: AST SpaceMobile Stock Pops. Thank AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile.
content.summary: AST SpaceMobile  stock was rising early Thursday.  A joint venture between major cell carriers to boost coverage in remote areas could be good news for the satellite-broadband stock.  AT&T Verizon Communications  and  T-Mobile US   said Thursday they are forming a joint venture to help end dead zones in the U.S. by the use of satellite-based wireless networks.

headline: US wireless carriers to launch joint venture to address rural 'dead zones'
summary: Verizon, AT&T and T-Mobile said on Thursday that they agreed in principle to form a new joint venture that seeks to address ‌long-time coverage gaps especially in rural areas by using satellite-based technologies.  The largest U.S. wireless ‌companies said the plan seeks to end nearly all dead zones without mobile service.  The plan also aims to ensure ​redundant connectivity during natural disasters and improved network performance using so-called "direct to device" satellite technology.

content.title: US wireless carriers to launch joint venture to address rural 'dead zones'
content.summary: Verizon, AT&T and T-Mobile said on Thursday that they agreed in principle to form a new joint venture that seeks to address ‌long-time coverage gaps especially in rural areas by using satellite-based technologies.  The largest U.S. wireless ‌companies said the plan seeks to end nearly all dead zones without mobile service.  The plan also aims to ensure ​redundant connectivity during natural disasters and improved network performance using so-called "direct to device" satellite technology.

headline: FCC approves Verizon's $1 billion spectrum purchase
summary: The Federal ‌Communications Commission ‌said Thursday it has ​approved Verizon's 2024 $1 billion deal to acquire some ‌spectrum assets ⁠from U.S. Cellular to expand ⁠its network capacity and ​coverage. Last year ​after ​T-Mobile acquired ‌most of U.S. Cellular's wireless operations and 30% of its wireless ‌spectrum in ​a $4.4 billion ​deal, ​the remainder ‌of the company ​changed ​its name to Array Digital ​Infrastructure. (Reporting ‌by David Shepardson, ​Editing by ​Franklin Paul)

content.title: FCC approves Verizon's $1 billion spectrum purchase
content.summary: The Federal ‌Communications Commission ‌said Thursday it has ​approved Verizon's 2024 $1 billion deal to acquire some ‌spectrum assets ⁠from U.S. Cellular to expand ⁠its network capacity and ​coverage. Last year ​after ​T-Mobile acquired ‌most of U.S. Cellular's wireless operations and 30% of its wireless ‌spectrum in ​a $4.4 billion ​deal, ​the remainder ‌of the company ​changed ​its name to Array Digital ​Infrastructure. (Reporting ‌by David Shepardson, ​Editing by ​Franklin Paul)

headline: Does Verizon’s Hybrid Bond Push And Debt Tenders Reshape The Bull Case For Verizon Communications (VZ)?
summary: Earlier this week, Verizon Communications announced a series of cash tender and exchange offers covering multiple high-coupon subsidiary debentures and notes, alongside issuing US$2.00 billion of fixed-to-floating, junior subordinated notes due 2058 to reshape its debt profile. By simultaneously retiring older subsidiary debt, loosening restrictive covenants, and replacing it with long-dated hybrid securities, Verizon is fine-tuning its balance sheet flexibility while maintaining access to...
